title: Restrict Egress to Namespace
description:
## Namespace-Scoped Egress Network Policy

Fine-grained network policies can restrict pod traffic so that only pods within a specific namespace can communicate with each other.

**Your task:**

A namespace `frontend` and a namespace `backend` already exist. Create a NetworkPolicy named `allow-backend-only` in the `frontend` namespace that:

- Applies to **all pods** in the `frontend` namespace
- **Allows egress only** to pods in the `backend` namespace (matched by `namespaceSelector`)
- **Denies all other egress** traffic

```bash
# Verify:
kubectl get networkpolicy allow-backend-only -n frontend
```

hints.title: namespaceSelector in Egress rule
hints.body: Use `spec.policyTypes: [Egress]` with an empty egress rule except for a `namespaceSelector` that matches the `backend` namespace label.
hints.command:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-backend-only
  namespace: frontend
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes:
  - Egress
  egress:
  - to:
    - namespaceSelector:
        matchLabels:
          kubernetes.io/metadata.name: backend
EOF

setup_commands.command: kubectl create namespace frontend --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
kubectl create namespace backend --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -

commands.command: kubectl get networkpolicy allow-backend-only -n frontend -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'
kubectl get networkpolicy allow-backend-only -n frontend -o jsonpath='{.spec.policyTypes[0]}'

commands.description: Policy uses namespaceSelector targeting backend namespace
